response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack web developer with strong software craftsmanship, but their background is fundamentally misaligned with this Principal Performance Architect role. They have no demonstrated experience in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator design, GPU architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services and some C/C++ tooling work, which falls far short of the hardware performance architecture expertise required at principal level.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years educational path
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance analysis experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or accelerator optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not hardware-adjacent performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN workload expertise
